text.title: Match Between System and Real World Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Match Between System and Real World. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every system term used in UI has a glossary mapping to an audience term.
rule.rationale: Every system term used in UI has a glossary mapping to an audience term.

rule.statement: Lists are ordered by the audience's mental model (e.g., 'Recent' before 'Archived'), not the system's storage order.
rule.rationale: Lists are ordered by the audience's mental model (e.g., 'Recent' before 'Archived'), not the system's storage order.

rule.statement: Dates, currency, numbers respect the user's locale.
rule.rationale: Dates, currency, numbers respect the user's locale.

rule.statement: Icons map to the audience's culture (no thumbs-up where it offends).
rule.rationale: Icons map to the audience's culture (no thumbs-up where it offends).

rule.statement: Error messages use the audience's idiom; no stack traces or internal error codes alone.
rule.rationale: Error messages use the audience's idiom; no stack traces or internal error codes alone.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-match-real-world.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
